## [00:00 - 03:02]

you know, you can't book any more and make any more sales because you only can take so many sales calls in a day. ## [12:17 - 15:18]

prematurely, what are the consequences here? So if we hire prematurely without a validated offer, one, the reps are not going to have any confidence that it's sellable. Okay, if they can't see you sell it, they're not going to think they can sell it. Number two, they and you will have no baseline to work off of in terms of what closing ratio should actually be. That is really problematic. Okay, it's going to make it hard to review calls, it's going to make it hard to give feedback, because you've never done it. You don't know if this is a 40% closing ratio type of offer. If this is a 20% closing ratio type of offer, you don't even know what a good call sounds like. When you're reviewing their calls, you have nothing to compare it to. It's all because you have no baseline. So you have to validate your offer and your sales process yourself and create that call library before you do so. I can maybe count less than three times I've ever seen anybody scale a sales team successfully without validating the offer and the sales process first. Now, if you hire without lead flow, one, you're probably going to get a shitty closer.
